message:
refactor(extensions): deterministic unit tests + async-join prompt pattern

Replace thread-based UI prompt simulation with futures::join! async
pattern in extension hostcall tests, eliminating thread spawn/join
overhead and potential flakiness from thread scheduling. The new pattern
uses asupersync::time::timeout to bound UI request waits and detect
unexpected extra prompts that would hang on future runs.

Introduce extension_manager_no_persisted_permissions() helper that
creates an ExtensionManager with permission_store set to None and
policy_prompt_cache cleared, ensuring unit tests are deterministic
and never mutate the user's global permissions file.

Affected tests:
- js_hostcall_prompt_mode_asks_once_per_capability
- js_hostcall_prompt_policy_caches_user_allow_and_never_logs_raw_params
  (fully restructured: thread → async join, adds timeout guards)
- hostcall_capability_prompt_all_builtin_capabilities_test
  (fully restructured: thread → async join, adds leak detection)

permissions.rs cleanups:
- Make list() and days_to_ymd() const fn
- Use is_none_or() instead of map_or(true, ...) for expiry check
- Collapse record() parameter formatting to single line
- Apply rustfmt to parse_owner_repo signature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
